name: ansible-builder

metadata.name: ansible-builder
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: "A tool for building Ansible Execution Environments"

documentation:
[![CI](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-builder/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=devel)](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-builder/actions?query=branch%3Adevel)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/ansible/ansible-builder/branch/devel/graph/badge.svg?token=4F6P3DBI40)](https://codecov.io/gh/ansible/ansible-builder)

# Ansible Builder

Ansible Builder is a tool that automates the process of building execution
environments using the schemas and tooling defined in various Ansible
Collections and by the user.

See the readthedocs page for `ansible-builder` at:

https://ansible-builder.readthedocs.io/en/stable/


## Get Involved:

* We use [GitHub issues](https://github.com/ansible/ansible-builder/issues) to
  track bug reports and feature ideas
* Want to contribute, check out our [guide](CONTRIBUTING.md)
* Join us in the `#ansible-builder` channel on Libera.chat IRC
* For the full list of Ansible email Lists, IRC channels and working groups,
  check out the [Ansible Mailing
  lists](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/community/communication.html#mailing-list-information)
  page of the official Ansible documentation.

## Code of Conduct

We ask all of our community members and contributors to adhere to the [Ansible
code of
conduct](http://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/community/code_of_conduct.html). If
you have questions, or need assistance, please reach out to our community team
at [codeofconduct@ansible.com](mailto:codeofconduct@ansible.com)

## License

[Apache License v2.0](./LICENSE.md)

functions.name: ansible_builder.ee_schema._handle_aliasing
documentation:
Upgrade EE keys into standard keys across schema versions.

Some EE keys are renamed across schema versions. So that we don't need to
check schema version, or do some other hackery, in the builder code when
accessing the values, just do the key name upgrades/aliasing here.

functions.name: ansible_builder.ee_schema._handle_options_defaults
documentation:
JSONSchema can document a "default" value, but it isn't used for validation.
This method is used to set any default values for the "options" dictionary
properties.

functions.name: ansible_builder.utils.copy_directory
documentation:
Recursively copy a source directory to a path in the context directory.

In order to not corrupt the build context cache, if it should exist, we
attempt to copy files within the source directory to the context directory
if necessary by utilizing copy_file() on each file, rather than a blind
recursive copy.

functions.name: ansible_builder.utils.copy_file
documentation:
Used to copy a source file to a destination file in the container runtime
context subfolder.

This utility function helps in maintaining the build cache. We only want
to copy the file if it doesn't exist, or if it has changed between builds.
See the `copy_directory()` function for the directory copy equivalent.

:param source str: Path to a source file.
:param dest str: Path to a destination file within the context subdir.
:param ignore_mtime bool: Whether or not mtime should be considered.

:returns: True if the file was copied, False if not.

:raises: Exception if called with the path to a directory. This helps to
    catch programming errors.

classes.name: ansible_builder.policies.SignedIdentityType
documentation:
Signature identity types as defined in:
https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-policy.json.5.md

methods.name: ansible_builder.policies.BaseImagePolicy.identity_type
documentation:
Returns the signed identity type enum. Use `value` attribute to get
the string representation.

methods.name: ansible_builder.policies.BaseImagePolicy.generate_policy
documentation:
Generates the podman policy data.

:returns: A dict representing the policy file data.

methods.name: ansible_builder.policies.BaseImagePolicy.write_policy
documentation:
Write the podman policy file.

:param str policy_file: Path to the policy file.

classes.name: ansible_builder.policies.RejectAll
documentation: Class used to generate a podman image validation policy that rejects any image.

classes.name: ansible_builder.policies.IgnoreAll
documentation: Class used to generate a podman image validation policy that accepts any image.

classes.name: ansible_builder.policies.ExactReference

methods.name: ansible_builder.policies.ExactReference.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ExactReference object.

:param str keypath: Path to the GPG keyring used to validate all images.

methods.name: ansible_builder.policies.ExactReference.add_image
documentation:
Add a new image signature name (and optional, original, non-mirrored
name) for this policy type.

:param str name: Complete image name to use in the policy file.
:param str sig_orig_name: If an image is mirrored, this is the complete
    original image name from the location being mirrored.

Class used to generate a podman image validation policy using the
'exactReference' signature identity type.

Example usage:

```
ref = ExactReference('/path/to/keyring.gpg')
ref.add_image('registry.redhat.io/aap-21/some-image:latest')
policy_data = ref.generate_policy()
```

methods.name: ansible_builder.main.AnsibleBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the AnsibleBuilder object.

:param str action: Builder action to perform (build/create/introspect).
:param str filename: Execution environment file to use.
:param dict build_args: Dictionary of build args to consider.
:param str build_context: Name of the build context directory.
:param list tag: List of tag names to apply to resulting image.
:param str container_runtime: Name of the container runtime in use.
:param str output_filename: Name of the resulting instruction file. If not supplied, it
    will default to a value based on container_runtime.
:param bool no_cache: If True, will not use the build cache when building an image.
:param bool prune_images: If True, will attempt an image prune at the end of a successful build.
:param int verbosity: Output verbosity level.
:param str galaxy_keyring: GPG keyring file used by ansible-galaxy to opportunistically
    validate collection signatures.
:param int galaxy_required_valid_signature_count: Number of sigs (prepend + to disallow no sig)
    required for ansible-galaxy to accept collections.
:param list galaxy_ignore_signature_status_codes: GPG Status code to ignore when validating galaxy collections.
:param str container_policy: The container validation policy. A valid string value from the PolicyChoices enum.
:param str container_keyring: GPG keyring for container image validation.
:param str squash: With podman, controls layer squashing.

methods.name: ansible_builder.main.AnsibleBuilder._handle_image_validation_opts
documentation:
Process the container_policy and container_keyring arguments.

:param str policy: The container_policy value.
:param str keyring: The container_keyring value.

The container_policy and container_keyring arguments come from the CLI
and work together to help build or use a podman policy.json file used
to do image validation. Depending on the policy being used, the keyring
may or may not be necessary.

The keyring, if required, must be a valid path, and will be transformed
to an absolute path to be used in the policy.json file.

:returns: A tuple of a PolicyChoices enum and abs path to the keyring.

functions.name: ansible_builder.cli.add_container_options
documentation: Add sub-commands and options relevant to containers.

classes.name: ansible_builder.cli.CustomVerbosityAction
documentation: Custom argparse Action to maintain backward compatibility for '-v N' and '-vvv'.

functions.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.read_req_file
documentation: Provide some minimal error and display handling for file reading

functions.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.process_collection
documentation:
Return a tuple of (python_dependencies, system_dependencies) for the
collection install path given.
Both items returned are a list of dependencies.

:param str path: root directory of collection (this would contain galaxy.yml file)

functions.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.process
ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.has_content
documentation:
Beyond checking that the candidate exists, this also assures
that the file has something other than whitespace,
which can cause errors when given to pip.

functions.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.strip_comments
documentation:
Filter any comments out of the Python collection requirements input.

```
param dict reqs

A dict of Python requirements, keyed by collection name.

return

Same as the input parameter, except with no comment lines.
```

functions.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.should_be_excluded
documentation:
Test if `value` matches against any value in `exclusion_list`.

The exclusion_list values are either strings to be compared in a case-insensitive
manner against value, OR, they are regular expressions to be tested against the
value. A regular expression will contain '~' as the first character.

```
return

True if the value should be excluded, False otherwise.
```

functions.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.filter_requirements
documentation:
Given a dictionary of Python requirement lines keyed off collections,
return a list of cleaned up (no source comments) requirements
annotated with comments indicating the sources based off the collection keys.

Currently, non-pep508 compliant Python entries are passed through. We also no
longer attempt to normalize names (replace '\_' with '-', etc), other than
lowercasing it for exclusion matching, since we no longer are attempting
to combine similar entries.

```
param dict reqs

A dict of either Python or system requirements, keyed by collection name.

param list exclude

A list of requirements to be excluded from the output.

param list exclude_collections

A list of collection names from which to exclude all requirements.

param bool is_python

This should be set to True for Python requirements, as each
will be tested for PEP508 compliance. This should be set to False for system requirements.

return

A list of filtered and annotated requirements.
```

classes.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.CollectionDefinition

methods.name: ansible_builder._target_scripts.introspect.CollectionDefinition.get_dependency
documentation:
A collection is only allowed to reference a file by a relative path
which is relative to the collection root

This class represents the dependency metadata for a collection
should be replaced by logic to hit the Galaxy API if made available

classes.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.ImageDescription

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.ImageDescription.__init__
documentation:
Create an object based on the 'images' portion of the EE definition file.

:param dict ee_images: The 'images' portion of the EE file.
:param str image_key: The section (one of 'base_image' or 'builder_image')
    of the 'images' dict to parse.

:raises: ValueError for an invalid image_key value (programmer error),
    or DefinitionError for invalid EE syntax or missing image tag.

Class to describe a container image from the EE file.

For the currently supported 'exactReference' type, this class is dead
simple. If we deem that we need to support more types, this class may
become more complex.

classes.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the UserDefinition object.

:param str filename: Path to the EE file. If this is None, we will
    look for the default file: execution-environment.(yml|yaml)

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition.version
documentation:
Integer version of the EE file.

If no version is specified, assume version 1 (for backward compat).

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition.ansible_config
documentation: Path to the user specified ansible.cfg file

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition.additional_build_steps
documentation: Gets additional commands from the exec env file, if any are specified.

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition.get_dep_abs_path
documentation:
Unique to the user EE definition, files can be referenced by either
an absolute path or a path relative to the EE definition folder
This method will return the absolute path.
This is the size of galaxy, python, system * exclude=True/False

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition._validate_additional_build_files
documentation:
Check that entries in additional_build_files look correct.

The 'dest' values are checked for the correct format. Since 'src' can
be a file glob or an absolute or relative path, it is not checked.

:raises: DefinitionError exception if any errors are found.

methods.name: ansible_builder.user_definition.UserDefinition.validate
documentation:
Check that all specified keys in the definition file are valid.

:raises: DefinitionError exception if any errors are found.
Class representing the Execution Environment file.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a Containerfile object for instruction file creation.

:param UserDefinition definition: Object describing the EE definition.
:param str build_context: Name of the build context subdirectory.
:param str container_runtime: Name of the container runtime in use.
:param str output_filename: Name of the resulting instruction file. If not supplied, it
    will default to a value based on container_runtime.
:param str galaxy_keyring: GPG keyring file used by ansible-galaxy to opportunistically
    validate collection signatures.
:param int galaxy_required_valid_signature_count: Number of sigs (prepend + to disallow no sig)
    required for ansible-galaxy to accept collections.
:param list galaxy_ignore_signature_status_codes: GPG Status codes to ignore when validating galaxy collections.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile.prepare
documentation:
Prepares the steps for the run-time specific build file.

Incrementally builds the `self.steps` attribute by extending it with the
info to eventually be written directly to the container definition file
via a separate call to the `Containerfile.write()` method.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile.write
documentation:
Writes the steps (built via the `Containerfile.prepare()` method) for
the runtime-specific build file (Dockerfile or Containerfile) to the
context directory.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile._insert_global_args
documentation:
Insert Containerfile ARGs and, possibly, their values.

An ARG with a None or empty value will not be included.

:param bool include_values: If True, include the ARG values in the directives.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile._create_folder_copy_files
documentation:
Creates the build context directory, and copies any potential context
files (python, galaxy, or bindep requirements) into it.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile._handle_additional_build_files
documentation:
Deal with any files the user wants added to the image build context.

The 'src' value is either an absolute path, or a path relative to the
EE definition file. For example, 'src' can be a relative path like
"data_files/configs/*.cfg", but cannot be "/home/user/files/*.cfg",
the latter not being relative to the EE.

methods.name: ansible_builder.containerfile.Containerfile._add_copy_for_file
documentation:
If the given file exists within the context build directory, add a COPY command to the
instruction file steps.

```
param str filename

The base requirement filename to check.

return

True if file exists and COPY command was added, False otherwise.
```
